THE WATERSPOUT STARTED IN HELMER BAY PORTION OF MANISTIQUE LAKE AND MOVED SOUTHWARD TOWARDS CURTIS.

GOGEBIC COUNTY POLICE REPORTED ROAD WASHED OUT JUST WEST OF LITTLE GIRLS POINT DUE TO STORMS THAT PASSED OVER EARLIER THIS AFTERNOON. ROAD HAS BEEN CLOSED BETWEEN LITTLE GIRLS POINT AND WEST TO THE WISCONSIN STATE LINE.

TIME WAS RADAR ESTIMATED. OTHER HAIL OBSERVED WAS BETWEEN 3/4 INCH AND 1 INCH.

APPROX. 2 FT DIAMETER PINE TREE UPROOTED IN BACKYARD ON ROCKLAND STREET. BROKEN OFF IN THE NORTH TO NORTHEAST DIRECTION. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.

MULTIPLE LOCATIONS ALONG CALEDONIA STREET HAD TREE DAMAGE. ONE HOUSE HAD A TREE BRANCH AT ABOUT 1.5 FT IN DIAMETER THAT FELL ONTO HIS DECK. VARIOUS OTHER 6 INCH TO 1 FT DIAMETER BRANCHES DOWN. ALL OF THE TREES WERE BROKEN OFF IN A NORTH TO NORTHEAST DIRECTION. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.

SMALL WOODEN SHED ROLLED ONCE OVER A FENCE AT CALUMET ELEMENTARY SCHOOL. SHED WAS ABOUT 8 FEET HIGH AND 12 FEET WIDE MADE OUT OF WOOD. HEAVY STRUCTURE. ROLLED TO THE NORTHEAST. PLAY STRUCTURE AT PARK WAS ALSO TIPPED OVER. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.

METAL BUILDING HOUSING A TRAVEL TRAILER DESTROYED. SEVERAL TREES ESTIMATED TO BE BETWEEN 1.5 TO 2 FEET IN DIAMETER WERE UPROOTED. DAMAGE BEGAN ON MINE STREET AND CONTINUED THROUGH THE INTERSECTION OF US41 AND CHURCH STREET IN NORTH CALUMET. DAMAGE PATH WAS ORIENTED SW TO NE.

GOLF COURSE SUSTAINED TREE DAMAGE. SEVERAL OAK TREES APPROXIMATELY 1 FT IN DIAMETER SNAPPED. SEVERAL PINE TREE BRANCHES BROKEN...APPROXIMATELY 6 INCHES IN DIAMETER. ONE PINE TREE WAS UPROOTED...APPOXIMATELY 1 FT IN DIAMETER. TREES FELL TOWARDS THE NORTH-NORTHEAST. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.

150 YARD STRETCH OF APPROXIMATELY 40 TREES UPROOTED OR SNAPPED RANGING FROM 6 INCHES TO AROUND 1.5 FEET IN DIAMETER. TREES FELL IN THE SAME GENERAL DIRECTION. SEVERAL OF THE TREES FELL ACROSS M26 CLOSING THE HIGHWAY.
62 MPH WIND GUST MEASURED AT STANNARD ROCK
TREES WERE REPORTED DOWN ON POWERLINES ALONG ANDERSON ROAD IN DAGGETT COUNTY BETWEEN 2100 AND 2130 CDT.
